61.reason: [This function does not return a value in all recursive paths. When recursing into AddNode(N->left, data) or AddNode(N->right, data), the return value is ignored and the function falls through to return ERROR, which is incorrect.]

72.reason: [The logic for identifying leaf nodes is wrong: it prints nodes that have at least one child missing (`!N->left || !N->right`), but leaf nodes must have both children NULL (`!N->left && !N->right`). Also, the traversal order is post-order, but the problem requires left-to-right (in-order) enumeration of leaves.]

Also, the traversal order is post-order, but the problem requires left-to-right (in-order) enumeration of leaves.]

88.reason: [If N is 0, this underflows and leads to reading negative number of inputs. Also, if N==0, the program should exit without doing anything, but here it proceeds incorrectly.]
